outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Hezbollah wounds 10 IDF soldiers; IRGC threatens US Gulf bases; Trump NSC meeting looms Tuesday
outlets.summary: CNN Day 80 coverage: Hezbollah rockets wound 8 IDF soldiers in northern Israel (including Finance Minister Smotrich's son) and anti-tank missile wounds 2 more. IRGC Navy threatens to deactivate all US Gulf bases. Trump Situation Room meeting May 19 is the most serious war-resumption decision point since the ceasefire. April CPI food prices up 0.7% MoM — largest jump in 4 years.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant as US and Iran signal they are prepared to resume war
outlets.summary: Washington Post frames the UAE Barakah drone strike and Trump's ultimatum as dual signals that both sides are positioning for potential return to active military operations. Analysts warn the May 19 NSC meeting could authorize strikes within days if Iran does not produce an acceptable counter-offer.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: US and Iran Far From Hormuz Deal as Drone Hits UAE Power Plant
outlets.summary: Bloomberg reports US and Iran remain far apart on Hormuz sovereignty terms. Brent crude $111.19/bbl (+39.3% vs pre-war). IRGC claims zero vessel transits May 18. UBS: if Hormuz reopens, Brent could ease to $100 by June.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran proxies hit UAE nuclear plant; Trump issues 'Clock is Ticking' ultimatum — Netanyahu-Trump coordinate on resumed war
outlets.summary: Fox News and Breitbart frame the Barakah drone strike as proof Iran-linked proxies are escalating across the Gulf, justifying Trump's ultimatum. Coverage highlights Trump-Netanyahu coordination, the IDF on alert for 'surprise scenarios,' and the Iran PGSA toll system as economic aggression.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran announces Hormuz toll mechanism — will charge ships, ban US 'Project Freedom' vessels
outlets.summary: Fox covers the PGSA announcement as an Iranian provocation, framing it as Iran monetizing its control of the Strait and directly targeting Trump's Project Freedom escort mission.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran drone hits UAE Barakah nuclear plant — no radiation; Trump calls for 'very bad time' for Iran
outlets.summary: Fox News covers the drone attack on the UAE's Barakah nuclear facility perimeter and Trump's warning to Iran of 'a very bad time.' Fox frames the attack as proof Iran is continuing its campaign of aggression despite the ceasefire and as justification for Trump's stance against a weak nuclear deal.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: UAE nuclear plant hit in first such strike of war; Iran formalizes $2M Hormuz tolls as Trump sets May 19 military options meeting
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera, BBC, and Reuters lead with the Barakah nuclear plant drone strike and the Iran PGSA launching formal operations. International coverage emphasizes the unprecedented nature of a nuclear facility being attacked and the IAEA monitoring team dispatch.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran's New Hormuz Authority Threatens to Cement Supply Disruption Beyond Any Ceasefire
outlets.summary: Bloomberg analysis warns that if the Persian Gulf Strait Authority becomes operational, it would structurally embed a Hormuz risk premium into global oil markets for years, regardless of when the conflict ends.

outlets.name: BBC World
outlets.headline: UAE nuclear plant hit by drone for first time; US and Israel weigh resumed strikes
outlets.summary: BBC World reports that a drone struck an electrical generator on the outer perimeter of the UAE's Barakah nuclear power plant, sparking a fire. UAE authorities confirmed no radiation release. No group claimed responsibility. BBC notes the attack comes as the US and Israel are in reported 'intense preparations' for possible renewed strikes on Iran, and as Lebanon reported 18 killed in Israeli strikes despite the ceasefire extension.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Netanyahu convenes security cabinet after Trump call; IDF on alert for 'surprise scenarios' as May 19 Situation Room meeting looms
outlets.summary: Times of Israel and Haaretz focus on Netanyahu's security cabinet meeting after his call with Trump, with both coordinating on possible resumed military action. Israeli media report IDF on alert for 'surprise scenarios.' The Barakah nuclear plant strike covered as demonstrating Iran-proxy recklessness.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Lebanon says 18 killed, 124 wounded in Israeli strikes over past day — as IDF hit 100 Hezbollah targets
outlets.summary: Haaretz reports Lebanon's Health Ministry counted 18 killed and 124 wounded in Israeli strikes in the 24 hours ending May 17. The IDF struck approximately 100 Hezbollah targets over the weekend despite the 45-day ceasefire extension. IDF also disclosed a weeklong Golani Brigade raid beyond the Litani River. Separately, a drone hit the UAE's Barakah nuclear plant perimeter — the first such attack.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: U.S. and Israel preparing for possible renewal of strikes on Iran as early as next week
outlets.summary: Haaretz broke the story on US-Israel 'intense preparations' for possible renewed strikes on Iran as early as next week, citing options including Kharg Island seizure and nuclear material extraction commando missions. The report signals diplomacy is effectively over unless Iran moves sharply.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi at BRICS: 'Iran was the victor' — Tehran warns UAE, announces stock market reopening May 19
outlets.summary: Press TV and IRNA lead with Araghchi's 'Iran won' declaration at BRICS, framing Iran as the stronger party in any resumed negotiations. Coverage highlights Iran's stock market reopening May 19 as a stabilization signal. The PGSA toll system presented as Iran exercising legitimate sovereignty over Hormuz.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Zionist-backed drone targets UAE Barakah reactor in escalation as PGSA Hormuz control takes effect
outlets.summary: Iranian state television Press TV frames the Barakah nuclear plant drone strike as a 'Zionist-backed' escalation. Press TV emphasizes the PGSA Hormuz toll mechanism now in effect, charging ships $2M per transit, and Araghchi's declaration that Iran was the 'victor' in the war.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Shamkhani: Iran ready to sign nuclear deal — if US removes all sanctions immediately
outlets.summary: Iran International (opposition channel) covers Shamkhani's conditional offer skeptically, noting the 'quick removal of all sanctions' condition is one Washington has repeatedly rejected as a starting point.
